title: MacTime v.1.4 November Update
description: In this huge update on MacTime, we're rolling out a brand new refreshed redesign, a bunch of powerful new features, important improvements, changes, and so much more! We haven't had a good update on MacTime for a while, so here's all the awesome stuff we're adding to MacTime coming your way:

mainarticles.p1: In this update, we're introducing a brand new focus mode that you can turn on in the Timer tab to turn your timer into a focus timer for work! If you remember, we had an individual tab called Focus for this functionality, but we figured that focus and timer work basically the same, just that focus has a break and more advanced features. To toggle the new focus mode in the Timer tab, simply click on the switch on the top right of the Timer tab and you can toggle focus mode on and off! With the introduction of focus mode, we're removing the old Focus tab. Along with this, we also fixed some bugs and made some improvements in the Timer tab 💪.
mainarticles.p2: We added a bunch of quality of life improvements and fixes to the countdown tab. First of all, we improved the code and math behind countdown to provide correct results when it comes to leap years. Second, we removed the month value in the countdown result and it's now days (e.g. 1 month and 3 days now shows 33 days). Note that this also counts in 30 day and 31 day months. Because of this change, when you're inputing the countdown date you have to write the date in this format: month/day. Other than that, we also added a notification sound when the countdown ends, added a new End button to end countdown early, and you can now directly start countdown just by pressing enter after inputing the values. Finaly, we improved the performance of countdown and some visual effects 🔥.
mainarticles.p3: This is probably the single biggest change across the entire MacWeb ecosystem. Because today, we're announcing MacWeb Site Material 5.0! With this brand new refined design system for MacWeb, the looks, user interfaces, and user experience will be completely refreshed and will be much more consitent, modern, easy to use, and appealing throughout MacWeb. With this new design on MacTime, it looks, feels, and works way more intuitive and it will be easier than ever to use MacTime for all your timing needs and make you more productive than ever! Not only that, the app now looks more updated, modern, and appealing to use 💡!
mainarticles.p4: Just like the new update schedule we're applying to other MacWeb apps, MacTime is also getting a new update schedule. From this update forward, MacTime is going to get an update every 3 months. This means we'll release a new update for MacTime each quarter of the year. Specifically, February, May, August, and November. Each update will be named after the season (Spring Update, Fall Update, etc). We made this change so the schedule is more consitent and regular and we have more time to develop exciting new features 📅!
mainarticles.p5: Other than all these awesome new changes above, we changed the old World Clock tab to the Time tab so it's more straigtforward and self explanatory. We made a new navigation bar with a new Tools dropdown containing the Timer and Countdown tabs so the tabs are more organized and easier to naviagate. We also fixed a bunch of bugs, optimized performance, and deleted and reorganized some files for MacTime 🔨.

others.other1: Because of Site Material 5.0 redesign, the modern update announcement modal has a brand new look to provide more information along with a thumbnail and a button for the update.
others.other2: Updated general appearance throughout the whole app.
others.other3: Minor appearance and functionality fixes on some tabs.
